learningObjective: Understand vectors as geometric objects with magnitude and direction that can be added and scaled. The aha moment is realizing that sailing directly into the wind is impossible, but you can reach upwind destinations by choosing angled approaches where your propulsion vector combines with the wind to produce a net motion toward the target—vectors don't just add numerically, their geometric relationship determines possibilities.
ahaMoment: Realizing that sailing directly into the wind is impossible (no sail angle works), but you can reach upwind destinations by choosing angled approaches where your propulsion vector combines with the wind to produce a net motion toward the target—vectors don't just add numerically, their geometric relationship determines possibilities

miniSpec.title: Wind-Assisted Sailboat Navigation Challenge
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: You're sailing a boat from island to island. A strong wind vector (shown as a large arrow) constantly pushes your boat, but you can rotate your sail to any angle to add your own propulsion vector. You must orient your sail direction and choose your sail power to reach the target island, working with or against the wind.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: The angle of the sail (rotating it in a full circle around the boat) and the sail tension setting (controlling the magnitude of the boat's self-propulsion vector), which together define the boat's propulsion vector that adds to the constant wind vector
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: As the sail rotates, a propulsion arrow extends from the boat in the sail's direction, its length controlled by sail tension; simultaneously, a resultant motion arrow (different color) shows the vector sum of wind plus propulsion, pointing where the boat will actually move
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Three distinct arrows are always visible from the boat: the fixed wind vector (blue), the rotating propulsion vector (green), and the resultant velocity vector (red) showing their sum; the parallelogram completing these vectors draws in faint dashed lines
miniSpec.trace_strategy: When the student commits to a choice, the boat animates along the resultant vector, leaving a wake trail; multiple attempt trails accumulate showing near-misses, creating a radial pattern that reveals how different sail angles produce different outcomes
miniSpec.aha_moment: Realizing that sailing directly into the wind is impossible (no sail angle works), but you can reach upwind destinations by choosing angled approaches where your propulsion vector combines with the wind to produce a net motion toward the target—vectors don't just add numerically, their geometric relationship determines possibilities

modelVariables.vectors_sailAngle: angle of the sail/propulsion vector in degrees (0-360), student-dragged via rotating handle
modelVariables.vectors_sailPower: magnitude of the propulsion vector (0-3), student-controlled via slider or scrubble
modelVariables.vectors_windX: x-component of the fixed wind vector
modelVariables.vectors_windY: y-component of the fixed wind vector
modelVariables.vectors_propulsionX: derived: x-component of propulsion vector = sailPower * cos(sailAngle)
modelVariables.vectors_propulsionY: derived: y-component of propulsion vector = sailPower * sin(sailAngle)
modelVariables.vectors_resultantX: derived: x-component of resultant = windX + propulsionX
modelVariables.vectors_resultantY: derived: y-component of resultant = windY + propulsionY
modelVariables.vectors_targetReached: derived: boolean, true when boat is within threshold of target island
modelVariables.vectors_isAnimating: boolean: whether boat is currently moving along resultant path

assertions.description: On load, the boat is not at the target island and resultant does not point directly at target - the puzzle is unsolved
assertions.visualCheck: Boat starts away from target island, three vectors visible from boat, target island clearly marked

assertions.description: When sail angle changes, the propulsion vector rotates accordingly
assertions.visualCheck: Green propulsion arrow rotates from pointing right to pointing up

assertions.description: Increasing sail power increases propulsion vector length
assertions.visualCheck: Green propulsion arrow grows longer while maintaining its direction

assertions.description: The resultant vector equals wind plus propulsion (the core vector addition concept)
assertions.visualCheck: Red resultant arrow points diagonally up-left, parallelogram dashed lines show vector addition geometry

assertions.description: When propulsion exactly opposes wind of same magnitude, resultant is zero (the aha moment for 'impossible' direction)
assertions.visualCheck: Resultant arrow shrinks to near-zero; boat cannot move directly upwind

assertions.description: An angled sail allows net motion with upwind component even against the wind
assertions.visualCheck: Resultant points up-left, showing net motion that has both leftward and upward components despite wind blowing right-to-left

invariants.description: Propulsion vector magnitude always equals sail power setting
Resultant always equals wind + propulsion (vector addition law)
